description: The GPIO module clock is disabled by default and must be enabled through the local Power and Sleep Controller (LPSC). The GPIO input clock runs at CPU/6, which defines timing for internal synchronization. Clock enable is a prerequisite for any register access or signal operation. Software must manage clock gating for power-performance trade-offs. Improper clock control will prevent interrupts and EDMA events from propagating.
dependencies: PSC clock enable (LPSC GPIO)
Remarks: Enable the GPIO LPSC before configuring direction or interrupts; disable only after quiescing activity.

feature_name: Two GPIO banks (2 x 16 signals, GP[31:0])
description: The device provides up to 32 GPIO signals organized into two banks of 16. Register files control a pair of banks using 32-bit-wide fields mapped per pin. Each pin maps to a consistent bit position across the banked registers. Bank organization is used for interrupt enables and status as well. Use the device data manual for package pin mapping details.
evidence: Section 2.2 Signal Descriptions; Section 2.5 GPIO Register Structure; Table 1
dependencies: PSC clock enable; Pin multiplexing
Remarks: Use bank numbers to determine the correct bit positions for configuration and status.

description: Extensive pin multiplexing is used to support many peripheral functions on the package. GPIO function availability per pin depends on hardware boot configuration and software-programmable mux registers. Correct muxing must be established before using a pin as GPIO. Misconfigured muxing will block GPIO control and observation. Refer to the device-specific data manual for mux register details.
dependencies: Device pinmux registers; PSC clock enable
Remarks: Apply pinmux first in the initialization sequence before DIR/interrupt setup.

description: GPIO operation is independent of processor endianness. Register layouts and bit meanings are identical in both little- and big-endian modes. No byte-lane swapping is needed for GPIO accesses. This simplifies low-level driver portability across endianness settings. No special handling is required in the GPIO driver.
dependencies: NONE
Remarks: Standard memory-mapped accesses suffice regardless of endianness.

description: Each GPIO pin can be individually configured as input or output using the DIR01 register. By default all pins reset as inputs (bit value 1). Clearing a DIR bit configures the pin as output; setting it configures as input. Direction must be set prior to driving or sampling the pin. Direction changes should be coordinated with external circuitry to avoid contention.
dependencies: PSC clock enable; Pin multiplexing
Remarks: Initialize DIR after pinmux; avoid switching direction on active lines without ensuring safe external states.

description: The OUT_DATA01 register holds the driven value for pins configured as outputs. Software can write the entire register (read-modify-write) to update multiple outputs at once. Reading OUT_DATA01 returns the register’s stored value, not necessarily the external pin state. For pins configured as inputs, writes to OUT_DATA01 have no effect. Use IN_DATA01 to observe actual signal levels.
dependencies: DIR configured as output for affected pins
Remarks: Prefer atomic SET/CLR registers for concurrency; use OUT_DATA01 when batch-updating many pins.

description: SET_DATA01 provides atomic set operations per pin to drive outputs high. Writing a 1 to a bit sets only that output without disturbing others, aiding multi-context control. Reading returns the output drive state of the corresponding pin. Writes do nothing on pins configured as inputs. This mechanism reduces the need for critical sections around GPIO updates.
dependencies: DIR configured as output for affected pins
Remarks: Use SET_DATA01 for single-bit high transitions to avoid read-modify-write races.

description: CLR_DATA01 provides atomic clear operations per pin to drive outputs low. Writing a 1 to a bit clears only that output, leaving others unchanged. Reading returns the output drive state of the corresponding pin. Writes are ignored on pins configured as inputs. Atomic clear complements SET_DATA01 for race-free low transitions.
dependencies: DIR configured as output for affected pins
Remarks: Use CLR_DATA01 for single-bit low transitions to avoid read-modify-write hazards.

description: IN_DATA01 returns the current state of each GPIO pin. For inputs, it reflects the signal synchronized to the GPIO clock domain; for outputs, it returns the device-driven output value. This allows software to sample debounced logic levels aligned with the peripheral clock. Open-drain behavior, if present on certain signals, affects the observed value due to wired-logic. Use the data manual to determine any open-drain pins.
evidence: Section 2.7.2 Reading a GPIO Input Signal; Section 3.7 IN_DATA01
dependencies: PSC clock enable; Direction configured as needed
Remarks: Sample IN_DATA01 in the intended clock context; external pull-ups/pull-downs influence open-drain readings.

description: Each GPIO pin supports independent rising, falling, both, or no-edge interrupt detection. Configuration uses SET_RIS_TRIG01/CLR_RIS_TRIG01 and SET_FAL_TRIG01/CLR_FAL_TRIG01 registers. Edge detection is synchronized to the GPIO clock domain to avoid metastability. Direction need not be input to generate an interrupt; software-driven outputs can stimulate interrupts for test. Proper ISR servicing is required to clear status and avoid missed events.
evidence: Section 2.10.3 Configuring GPIO Interrupt Edge Triggering; Sections 3.8–3.11
dependencies: PSC clock enable; BINTEN bank enable; CPU interrupt routing
Remarks: Configure only one edge or both as required; ensure status is cleared after handling to re-arm detection.

description: Bank-level interrupt gating is provided per 16-pin bank via BINTEN. Setting EN0 or EN1 enables all configured pin interrupts within the respective bank to reach the DSP CPU. Clearing disables propagation while leaving per-pin edge configuration intact. The same bits also gate EDMA event generation. Use device interrupt mapping to connect bank sources to CPU vectors.
dependencies: Per-pin edge registers set; CPU interrupt controller configured
Remarks: Enable only banks in use to minimize spurious CPU wakeups; coordinate with EDMA event needs.

description: INTSTAT01 reports pending GPIO interrupts per pin with a write-one-to-clear (W1C) mechanism. Reading identifies which pins in each bank have active events. Writing a 1 to a bit clears the corresponding pending condition; writing 0 has no effect. Software must ensure all asserted bits are cleared to prevent retriggering. For direct CPU-routed GPIO interrupts, CPU flag registers also indicate status.
evidence: Section 2.10.4 GPIO Interrupt Status; Section 3.12 INTSTAT01
dependencies: BINTEN enabled; Per-pin edges configured
Remarks: Always clear only the handled bits using W1C semantics; read-modify-write is unnecessary and discouraged.

description: The GPIO peripheral can provide synchronization events to the EDMA controller. Each pin’s configured edges can act as EDMA triggers when the bank is enabled. This enables low-latency, CPU-offloaded responses to external signals. Event routing details and channel assignment are defined in the device data manual. Proper configuration allows efficient data movement synchronized to GPIO activity.
evidence: Section 1.2 Features; Section 2.11 EDMA Event Support
dependencies: BINTEN bank enable; EDMA event/channel mapping; PSC clock enable
Remarks: Consult the device EDMA event table to bind GPIO events to channels; avoid saturating EDMA with noisy lines.

description: GPIO supports reduced-power states controlled by the PSC. When placed in a low-power state, interrupt generation is suspended until the module is re-enabled. Output pins retain their driven states while the module is powered down. Proper sequencing is needed when entering and exiting low power to avoid missed edges. Power control interacts with clock gating policies for energy savings.
dependencies: PSC power state transitions; BINTEN/edge configs preserved as documented
Remarks: Quiesce interrupts and record state before gating power; re-enable and resync after wake.

description: A defined initialization flow is required after hardware reset. The steps are: configure pin multiplexing, enable the GPIO module via PSC, and then program direction, data, and interrupt control registers. Following this order ensures proper ownership of pins and valid clocking. Skipping steps may yield inoperative GPIO or bus faults. After initialization, the GPIO is ready for data transactions.
dependencies: PSC clock enable; Pin multiplexing; DIR/edge configuration
Remarks: Adhere strictly to the ordering: pinmux -> PSC enable -> configuration to avoid glitches and contention.

description: Software reset (emulator) does not modify GPIO signal configuration or state. A PSC reset cycle returns registers to default settings, as does hardware reset. Hardware reset initializes pins to default directions and data states. Understanding reset sources is key to predictable startup. Plan reinitialization accordingly after hardware or PSC resets.
dependencies: Reset source handling in system; Re-run initialization
Remarks: After PSC/hardware reset, reapply pinmux and direction; do not assume prior GPIO states persist.

description: GPIO is not affected by emulation suspend events such as halts and breakpoints. Pin states and internal edge detection continue according to hardware timing. This can be useful for observing real-world behavior during debug sessions. However, interrupts may occur while the CPU is halted. Plan debug procedures to avoid unintended system actions during halts.
dependencies: Debugger present; CPU halt conditions
Remarks: Mask or reroute critical interrupts when halting the CPU to prevent side effects.

feature_name: No interrupt multiplexing with other functions
description: GPIO interrupt sources are not multiplexed with other peripheral interrupt functions. This simplifies routing and avoids ambiguous sources. It ensures dedicated lines for GPIO-generated requests. System software need only manage bank enables and CPU vectors. Documentation in the device manual provides the exact interrupt numbers.
dependencies: CPU interrupt controller setup; BINTEN
Remarks: Use fixed mappings to simplify ISR registration and reduce configuration errors.

description: The PID register exposes peripheral identification including function code and revision. Major and minor revisions identify the GPIO IP version as 1.5 for this document. Reading PID helps software adapt to potential IP differences or errata workarounds. The scheme and custom fields are fixed for this peripheral. Use on startup to log hardware identity.
dependencies: PSC clock enable (to access registers)
Remarks: Capture PID during driver init for diagnostics and compatibility checks.

description: GPIO inputs are synchronized to the GPIO clock domain using internal flip-flops. This reduces metastability when sampling asynchronous external signals. The synchronized sampling underlies interrupt edge detection reliability. Designers should still observe external signal integrity and timing requirements. Excessive input jitter may produce multiple edge detections if not filtered externally.
evidence: Figure 1 GPIO Peripheral Block Diagram; Section 2.7.2
dependencies: Stable GPIO clock; Clean external signals
Remarks: Add external filtering or debounce as needed; the module does not provide built-in debounce.

description: Because edge detection does not require input direction, software can toggle a GPIO configured as output to generate its own interrupt. This is useful for verifying interrupt routing and ISR logic without external stimulus. It aids bring-up and board validation. Ensure BINTEN and per-pin edges are enabled. Clear INTSTAT01 after testing to restore normal operation.
dependencies: BINTEN enabled; Edge configs set; Direction set to output
Remarks: Use controlled test pulses and ensure ISRs handle and clear status to avoid retrigger storms.

description: Some signals may utilize open-drain output buffers for wired-logic operations as defined in the device data manual. In such cases, IN_DATA01 reflects the wired-logic level, not solely the device’s drive. External pull-ups or pull-downs determine the idle state. The GPIO guide defers exact pin lists to the data manual. Treat readings accordingly when open-drain is in use.
dependencies: Device data manual for pin capabilities; External pulls
Remarks: Confirm pin electrical type before enabling outputs to avoid bus contention on shared lines.
